scope.summary: D2 changed-line coverage ratchet made real: the leg's pathspec selected nothing, so it reported 'trivially green' on every diff ever measured; it now measures the diff, fails uncovered changed lines, and the lane attests only the legs that actually ran.
out_of_scope: The mutation-floor leg's ENFORCEMENT. It remains advisory this train under ALLOW_STALE_MUTATION_SEAL (operator ruling 2026-07-21, plan v8 §Z2); docs/quality/mutation-safety-d2.md does not exist yet and the seal is bead oraclemcp-091-rc-mutation-seal-5aqwf. This close makes that deferral HONEST (attested SKIP, never PASS); it does not lift it.
The evaluator itself (scripts/coverage_ratchet.py) and the D1 baseline (bead 5.1, closed): both were already correct and are unchanged.
docs/test-tiers.md §7, which still describes the mutation-floor leg as required rather than deferred. The file was under another agent's active lease at close time; tracked as oraclemcp-f7572.
The driver repo half of D2.

entry_points.name: scripts/coverage_ratchet.sh run --base <sha>
entry_points.trace: Real instrumented run (cargo llvm-cov -p oraclemcp-error, under scripts/build_lease.sh, disk-backed CARGO_TARGET_DIR, in a clean worktree at HEAD). NEGATIVE: four added lines with no test -> 'FAIL -- changed-line coverage below the floor', table 'oraclemcp-error 4 changed 0 covered 0.0% floor 80.0% FAIL' naming crates/oraclemcp-error/src/fuzzy.rs:170-173, exit 1. The SAME diff before the pathspec fix printed 'no changed crates/<crate>/src/*.rs lines -- changed-line leg trivially green', exit 0. POSITIVE: adding a test that calls the function -> '7 changed 7 covered 100.0% PASS', 'changed-line leg OK', exit 0, status file changed_line=enforced.

entry_points.name: scripts/coverage_ratchet.sh attestation-outcomes
entry_points.trace: Derives the lane's attested outcomes from what ran. After the enforced run: 'coverage-ratchet:changed-line-floor=PASS' + 'coverage-ratchet:mutation-floor=SKIP'. After a --check run with no diff: both SKIP. ci.yml's attestation step now consumes this instead of the hard-coded 'changed-line-floor=PASS / mutation-floor=PASS' pair; the emitter accepts PASS|SKIP|FAIL (crates/oraclemcp-verifier/src/bin/oraclemcp-test-attest.rs:142-145).

entry_points.name: scripts/coverage_ratchet.sh --self-test
entry_points.trace: Three suites, exit 0: the existing evaluator fixtures; a new pathspec test that builds a temp repo and asserts the pathspec selects crates/demo/src/lib.rs, excludes tests/, AND that the old 'crates/*/src' shape still selects nothing (so a simplification back to it fails); and the status->outcome mapping (deferred attests SKIP, enforced attests PASS, no-changed-lines attests SKIP, an absent status file fails closed). scripts/mutation_safety_gate.sh self-test additionally asserts both STATUS tokens and stays green (run from a clean worktree; see limitations).

limitations: The mutation gate's self-test cannot pass in a dirty shared checkout: scope_state hashes the WORKTREE, so another agent's uncommitted crates/oraclemcp-db/src edits raise E_SEAL_SOURCE_MISMATCH. That is correct staleness detection, not a defect; every self-test result cited here was produced from a clean worktree at HEAD.

The instrumented proof used oraclemcp-error (a leaf crate) to keep one build-lease slot occupied briefly among five agents. The safety-crate 90% floor is exercised by the evaluator's committed fixtures, not by a second live run.
No CI run was dispatched from this close. The ci.yml wiring is verified by parsing the workflow (no duplicate keys, step order correct) and by running the exact command sequence locally.

Consequence to expect, stated plainly: this lane was vacuous and is now enforcing, so the next push whose crates/<crate>/src lines are under-covered will fail a job that used to pass unconditionally. Recent history is a small blast radius (one oraclemcp-db source file across the last 12 commits).
